message:
Unified parse() API: return status-based ParseResult instead of throwing

Replace the throwing parse() with a status-based API that returns
{status, value, remaining?, error?} where status is one of
"complete", "complete_early", "incomplete", or "invalid". This enables
graceful handling of partial JSON (e.g. AI SDK token-by-token output)
without a separate streaming API.

Zig engine additions:
- classify_input: lightweight O(n) structural scanner (bracket depth,
  string state, escape tracking) that classifies input without parsing
- autocomplete_input: appends closing tokens (quotes, nulls, braces)
  to incomplete JSON in-place in the WASM buffer
- get_value_end: returns byte offset where the first complete value
  ends, for complete_early slicing

zimdjson string metadata refactor:
- Move per-string metadata (input offset, raw length, has_escapes) to
  a separate string_meta array indexed by ordinal, replacing the
  inline header approach for cleaner separation
- Store full string length in a single u32 header instead of split
  u16+u24 encoding

All existing tests updated: parse() calls now access .value; error
tests check status instead of catching SyntaxError. New 103-test suite
covers all four statuses, edge cases, proxy behavior, and stress tests.

         "doc_batch_ptr",
         "doc_array_elements",

+        "doc_f64_batch_ptr",
+        "doc_read_column_f64",
+        "doc_read_column_bool",
+        "doc_read_column_str_raw",
+        "doc_read_string_raw",
+        "doc_read_all_columns",
+        // Input classification & autocomplete
+        "classify_input",
+        "autocomplete_input",
+        "get_value_end",
         // Doc stringify (tape → JSON bytes, one call)
         "doc_stringify",
         // Eager materialization (tape → flat binary buffer, one call)
